items.q: Where can I store my luggage in Berlin?
items.a: Four reliable options: Deutsche Bahn lockers at major stations (Hauptbahnhof, Friedrichstrasse, Zoo, Ostbahnhof, Alexanderplatz, Suedkreuz, Potsdamer Platz, Gesundbrunnen), the staffed Gepäckcenter inside Berlin Hauptbahnhof, the luggage storage at BER airport's Terminal 1, and app-based shop networks like Bounce, Radical Storage, and Stasher.

items.q: How much does it cost to store luggage in Berlin?
items.a: At Berlin Hauptbahnhof, DB currently lists small 24-hour lockers at €4 and large 24-hour lockers at €6, with cheaper 2-hour options also listed. Other station, city, airport and app-based options vary, so treat the price as route-specific, not one universal Berlin luggage price.

items.q: Are there luggage lockers at Berlin Hauptbahnhof?
items.a: Yes. Berlin Hauptbahnhof has the strongest official locker setup in central Berlin. DB currently lists 616 small 24-hour lockers and 396 large 24-hour lockers there, plus 2-hour options. Bring a coin/payment backup and do not leave the decision until the last minute on a busy summer day.

items.q: Can I store luggage at Berlin BER airport?
items.a: Yes. BER lists baggage storage at Terminal 1, train station level U1. It is most useful if the airport remains part of your day. If your whole plan is in central Berlin, a station locker, hotel storage or city luggage-storage point usually avoids an unnecessary airport return.

items.q: Can I bring my suitcase on a Berlin walking tour?
items.a: A small backpack or day bag is fine on the Berlin Free Walking Tour. A full-size suitcase is not practical because of cobblestones, stairs, and crowded crossings. Drop the suitcase in a Hauptbahnhof locker or at the Gepäckcenter and take the U-Bahn one stop to Alexanderplatz to meet bag-free.
